agents.capPhiProtectionDesc: Never discusses or exposes Protected Health Information. Redirects requests for PHI to authorized personnel.
agents.capTaskCreationDesc: Allows this agent to autonomously create, assign, and track tasks for itself or other agents based on conversation context.

agents.phiAlwaysEnforcedDesc: Agents will never discuss, transmit, or expose Protected Health Information (PHI). When PHI is requested, the agent automatically redirects the requester to authorized clinical staff. This is a hard-coded safety parameter that cannot be overridden. Scheduling agents only confirm visit times without disclosing clinical details.
agents.securityZoneDesc: Determines what data, channels, and integrations this agent can access.
agents.subtitle: Create, customize, and deploy clinical AI agents for your practice
agents.templatesDesc: Pre-configured agent blueprints. Deploy one in a single click.
agents.title: Healthcare AI Agents

agents.zoneClinicalDesc: Handles protected health information. Internal platform communication only. No email, phone, SMS, or external endpoints.
agents.zoneClinicalRestrictions: No email/phone/SMS access,No external integrations,Cannot communicate with Zone 3 agents,EHR/EMR access only
agents.zoneOperationsDesc: Internal operations only. Receives de-identified data from Zone 1 through sanitization gate. No direct PHI access.
agents.zoneOperationsRestrictions: No direct PHI access,De-identified data only from Zone 1,Internal tools only
agents.zoneExternalDesc: Communicates with patients and external services. Email, SMS, voice, web enabled. Never has access to PHI data.
agents.zoneExternalRestrictions: Zero PHI access,Cannot communicate with Zone 1 agents,Public-facing channels enabled
agents.zone1RestrictionsActiveDesc: Clinical zone agents are restricted to internal platform communication only. Email, SMS, voice, and external messaging capabilities are automatically disabled. This agent can only communicate with other Zone 1 (Clinical) agents.

terms.contactQuestion: Questions about these terms? Contact us at legal@drclaw.ai
terms.section10Content1: All intellectual property rights in and to the Dr. Claw platform, including but not limited to software, algorithms, models, user interfaces, designs, trademarks, trade names, logos, and documentation, are and shall remain the exclusive property of Dr. Claw. Nothing in these Terms grants you any right, title, or interest in the platform's intellectual property except for the limited right to use the Service in accordance with these Terms.
terms.section10Content2: All data that you upload, input, or generate through the Service ("User Data") remains your sole property. Dr. Claw does not claim ownership of your User Data and will not use it for purposes other than providing and improving the Service, except as otherwise described in our Privacy Policy.
terms.section10Content3: Agent configurations, custom workflows, skill definitions, and other customizations created by you through the platform ("Custom Configurations") are your property. You retain full ownership of and rights to your Custom Configurations.
terms.section11Content1: Either party may terminate this agreement at any time by providing written notice to the other party. You may terminate your account at any time through your account settings or by contacting our support team. Dr. Claw may terminate or suspend your access to the Service immediately, without prior notice or liability, for any reason whatsoever, including without limitation if you breach these Terms.
terms.section11Content2: Upon termination, a data export period of thirty (30) days will be provided, during which you may download and export your User Data and Custom Configurations. After this 30-day period, Dr. Claw reserves the right to permanently delete your data from its systems, except as required by law or legitimate business purposes.
terms.section11Content3: Dr. Claw reserves the right to suspend or restrict access to accounts that violate these Terms, engage in fraudulent activity, pose a security risk to the platform or other users, or fail to pay applicable fees.
terms.section12Content: These Terms shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware, United States of America, without regard to its conflict of law provisions. Any legal action or proceeding arising out of or relating to these Terms or the Service shall be brought exclusively in the federal or state courts located in the State of Delaware, and you hereby consent to the personal jurisdiction and venue of such courts. You waive any objection to the exercise of jurisdiction over you by such courts and to venue in such courts.
terms.section13Content: Dr. Claw reserves the right to modify, amend, or update these Terms at any time at its sole discretion. We will provide at least thirty (30) days' advance notice of any material changes by posting the updated Terms on the platform, sending an email notification to the address associated with your account, or through other reasonable means of communication. Your continued use of the Service after the effective date of the revised Terms constitutes your acceptance of and agreement to the updated Terms. If you do not agree to the modified Terms, you must discontinue use of the Service before the changes take effect.
terms.section1Content: By accessing or using the Dr. Claw platform, including any associated services, features, content, or applications (collectively, the "Service"), you acknowledge that you have read, understood, and agree to be bound by these Terms of Service & Conditions ("Terms"). If you do not agree to these Terms, you must not access or use the Service. These Terms constitute a legally binding agreement between you ("User," "you," or "your") and Dr. Claw ("Company," "we," "us," or "our"). Your continued use of the platform following the posting of any changes to these Terms constitutes acceptance of those changes.
terms.section2Content: Dr. Claw provides an AI agent platform designed to streamline and automate business operations, with specialized capabilities for healthcare settings. The Service enables users to deploy, configure, and manage AI-powered agents that assist with tasks including but not limited to scheduling, patient communication, data management, workflow automation, and operational analytics. The platform is intended to augment human decision-making and operational efficiency, not to replace professional judgment. Features, functionality, and availability of the Service may change at any time without prior notice at the sole discretion of Dr. Claw.
terms.section3Content: As a user of the Service, you agree to: (a) provide accurate, current, and complete information during registration and throughout your use of the platform; (b) maintain the security and confidentiality of your account credentials, including passwords, API keys, and access tokens; (c) promptly notify Dr. Claw of any unauthorized use of your account or any other breach of security; (d) not use the Service for any unlawful purpose or in any way that could damage, disable, overburden, or impair the platform; (e) not attempt to gain unauthorized access to any portion of the Service, other accounts, computer systems, or networks connected to the Service; (f) not use the Service to transmit any viruses, malware, or other harmful code; and (g) comply with all applicable local, state, national, and international laws and regulations in connection with your use of the Service.
terms.section4Bullet1: User negligence, including but not limited to weak passwords, sharing credentials, or failure to enable multi-factor authentication;
terms.section4Bullet2: Third-party integrations, services, or applications that the user connects to the platform;
terms.section4Bullet3: Unauthorized sharing of account credentials, API keys, or access tokens by the user or the user's personnel;
terms.section4Bullet4: Force majeure events, including but not limited to natural disasters, acts of war, terrorism, cyberattacks by nation-state actors, pandemics, or other events beyond Dr. Claw's reasonable control.
terms.section4Content1: Dr. Claw implements industry-standard security measures to protect user data, including AES-256 encryption for data at rest, TLS 1.3 for data in transit, and SOC 2 Type II compliance across our infrastructure. We maintain rigorous access controls, regular security audits, continuous monitoring, and multi-layered defense protocols to safeguard the integrity and confidentiality of your information.
terms.section4Content2: Notwithstanding the foregoing, Dr. Claw shall NOT be held liable for data breaches caused by:
terms.section5Content1: Dr. Claw provides HIPAA-eligible infrastructure designed to support the handling of Protected Health Information (PHI) in compliance with the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act of 1996, as amended, and its implementing regulations. Users who handle PHI through the platform are required to execute a Business Associate Agreement (BAA) with Dr. Claw prior to processing, storing, or transmitting any PHI through the Service.
terms.section5Content2: Dr. Claw is not responsible for PHI exposure, unauthorized disclosure, or data breaches caused by user misconfiguration of AI agents, workflows, or integrations. It is the user's sole responsibility to ensure that their agent configurations, access controls, and data handling practices comply with HIPAA requirements. Users must conduct their own risk assessments and implement appropriate administrative, physical, and technical safeguards in conjunction with the platform's built-in security features.
terms.section6Bullet1: Any indirect, incidental, consequential, special, exemplary, or punitive damages, including but not limited to loss of profits, revenue, goodwill, or data;
terms.section6Bullet2: Loss of data due to third-party services, integrations, or infrastructure providers;
terms.section6Bullet3: AI agent outputs, recommendations, or decisions made without appropriate human oversight and review;
terms.section6Bullet4: Data breaches resulting from the user's failure to implement recommended security practices, configurations, or updates provided by Dr. Claw.
terms.section6Content1: TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY APPLICABLE LAW, DR. CLAW'S MAXIMUM AGGREGATE LIABILITY ARISING OUT OF OR RELATED TO THESE TERMS OR THE SERVICE SHALL BE LIMITED TO THE TOTAL FEES PAID BY YOU TO DR. CLAW DURING THE TWELVE (12) MONTHS IMMEDIATELY PRECEDING THE EVENT GIVING RISE TO THE CLAIM.
terms.section6Content2: In no event shall Dr. Claw be liable for:
terms.section7Content1: AI agents deployed through the Dr. Claw platform are tools designed to assist and augment human decision-making. They are not intended to replace professional human judgment in any capacity. Users are solely responsible for reviewing, verifying, and approving all agent outputs, recommendations, and actions before relying upon or implementing them.
terms.section7Content2: Dr. Claw does not guarantee the accuracy, completeness, reliability, or timeliness of any AI-generated content, analysis, or recommendations. AI agents may produce incorrect, incomplete, or outdated information, and users should exercise independent judgment and professional expertise when evaluating agent outputs.
terms.section7Content3: Healthcare-related AI agents available through the platform do not provide medical advice, diagnoses, or treatment recommendations. They are administrative and operational tools only. Any health-related information generated by the platform should not be considered a substitute for professional medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment from a qualified healthcare provider.
terms.section8Bullet1: Your use or misuse of the Service;
terms.section8Bullet2: Your violation of these Terms or any applicable law or regulation;
terms.section8Bullet3: Your violation of any third-party rights, including intellectual property rights, privacy rights, or contractual obligations;
terms.section8Bullet4: Data breaches resulting from your negligence, misconfiguration, or failure to implement adequate security measures.
terms.section8Content: You agree to indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Dr. Claw, its officers, directors, employees, agents, affiliates, successors, and assigns from and against any and all claims, damages, losses, liabilities, costs, and expenses (including reasonable attorneys' fees) arising out of or related to:
terms.section9Content1: In the event of a confirmed data breach affecting user data, Dr. Claw will notify affected users within seventy-two (72) hours of discovering the breach, in accordance with applicable data breach notification laws and regulations, including but not limited to HIPAA breach notification requirements and state-level data breach notification statutes.
terms.section9Content2: Dr. Claw maintains comprehensive incident response procedures, including a dedicated security incident response team, documented escalation protocols, forensic investigation capabilities, and remediation processes designed to contain, assess, and resolve security incidents promptly and effectively.
terms.section9Content3: Dr. Claw will cooperate fully with law enforcement investigations related to data breaches or security incidents affecting the platform or its users, to the extent permitted by applicable law and consistent with our obligations to protect user privacy.
terms.title: Terms of Service & Conditions

training.subtitle: Master the Dr. Claw platform with comprehensive step-by-step tutorials — from basic setup to advanced agent configuration
training.title: Training Center
training.tutorialAgentSettingsDesc: Fine-tune agent behavior with advanced settings — including response rules, escalation paths, operating hours, and skill priorities.
training.tutorialAgentSettingsStep1: Open an existing agent from the Healthcare Agents page and click the Settings tab
training.tutorialAgentSettingsStep2: Set operating hours to define when the agent is active vs. sending after-hours responses
training.tutorialAgentSettingsStep3: Configure escalation rules — define when the agent should hand off to a human (e.g., urgent symptoms, billing disputes)
training.tutorialAgentSettingsStep4: Adjust response length (concise, standard, detailed) and tone (professional, friendly, clinical)
training.tutorialAgentSettingsStep5: Set skill priorities to control which capabilities the agent uses first when handling requests
training.tutorialAgentSettingsStep6: Enable or disable specific communication channels (chat, email, SMS, voice)
training.tutorialAgentSettingsStep7: Save your configuration and test the changes in the Agent Playground
training.tutorialApiPortalDesc: Access the API portal to generate keys, test endpoints, configure webhooks, and integrate Dr. Claw capabilities into your custom applications.
training.tutorialApiPortalStep1: Navigate to API Portal and generate a new API key for your application
training.tutorialApiPortalStep2: Review the API documentation — explore available endpoints for agents, patients, tasks, and communications
training.tutorialApiPortalStep3: Test API calls directly from the interactive endpoint explorer using your generated key
training.tutorialApiPortalStep4: Configure webhooks to receive real-time notifications when specific events occur (new patient, task completed, etc.)
training.tutorialApiPortalStep5: Monitor API usage and rate limits from the usage dashboard to ensure you stay within your plan allocation
training.tutorialBillingDesc: Manage your Dr. Claw subscription, view usage metrics, update payment methods, and understand your billing cycle.
training.tutorialBillingStep1: Navigate to Billing & Plans to see your current subscription tier and usage summary
training.tutorialBillingStep2: Review the pricing comparison to understand what's included in each tier (Starter, Professional, Enterprise)
training.tutorialBillingStep3: Update your payment method or billing address from the Payment section
training.tutorialBillingStep4: Download past invoices from the Billing History table for your accounting records
training.tutorialBillingStep5: Monitor agent usage metrics to track API calls, message volume, and storage consumption against your plan limits
training.tutorialCommandStationDesc: Master the real-time Command Station — communicate with agents, issue instructions, and monitor all agent activity from a single control panel.
training.tutorialCommandStationStep1: Navigate to Command Station to see the live agent hierarchy and activity overview
training.tutorialCommandStationStep2: Select an agent to open the direct command chat — issue instructions or ask for status updates
training.tutorialCommandStationStep3: Monitor the real-time activity log to see what each agent is currently processing
training.tutorialCommandStationStep4: Use the metrics panel to view agent response times, task completion rates, and error counts
training.tutorialCommandStationStep5: Issue bulk commands to multiple agents simultaneously using the broadcast feature
training.tutorialCommunicationDesc: Master the zone-isolated messaging system — manage patient conversations, inter-agent messaging, and internal team communications securely.
training.tutorialCommunicationStep1: Navigate to Patient Comms and review the three communication zones: Patient, Internal, and Partner
training.tutorialCommunicationStep2: Open a patient conversation thread to see how agents handle incoming messages with suggested responses
training.tutorialCommunicationStep3: Use the inter-agent messaging panel to route tasks between agents (e.g., Front Desk to Billing Navigator)
training.tutorialCommunicationStep4: Configure auto-response templates for common patient inquiries (appointment confirmations, prescription refills)
training.tutorialCommunicationStep5: Review the message audit trail to verify all communications comply with HIPAA zone isolation rules
training.tutorialCreateAgentDesc: Learn how to create, name, and configure a healthcare AI agent from scratch — including role assignment, zone placement, and personality settings.
training.tutorialCreateAgentStep1: Navigate to Healthcare Agents from the sidebar and click 'Create New Agent'
training.tutorialCreateAgentStep2: Choose a name and avatar for your agent (e.g., 'Dr. Front Desk' for reception tasks)
training.tutorialCreateAgentStep3: Select the agent's role: Medical Receptionist, Clinical Assistant, Billing Navigator, or Custom
training.tutorialCreateAgentStep4: Assign the agent to a security zone — Zone 1 (Clinical), Zone 2 (Administrative), or Zone 3 (External)
training.tutorialCreateAgentStep5: Configure the agent's personality tone, response length, and escalation rules, then activate it
training.tutorialDataLogsDesc: Review real-time system logs to track agent activity, data access events, API calls, and security incidents for compliance reporting.
training.tutorialDataLogsStep1: Navigate to Audit & Data Logs to see the real-time log stream with color-coded severity levels
training.tutorialDataLogsStep2: Use category filters to isolate specific log types: Communication, Task, API, System, Auth, or Integration
training.tutorialDataLogsStep3: Search logs by keyword to quickly find specific events (e.g., a patient name or error code)
training.tutorialDataLogsStep4: Review the log statistics panel to see total events, error rate, and most active agents
training.tutorialDataLogsStep5: Export filtered logs as CSV for compliance documentation or incident investigation
training.tutorialDeployTemplatesDesc: Quickly launch production-ready agents using Dr. Claw's curated templates — pre-configured for common healthcare workflows.
training.tutorialDeployTemplatesStep1: Go to Healthcare Agents and browse the Template Gallery to see available pre-built agents
training.tutorialDeployTemplatesStep2: Preview a template (e.g., 'Insurance Verifier') to review its included skills, zone, and default settings
training.tutorialDeployTemplatesStep3: Click 'Deploy Template' and customize the agent name and greeting message for your practice
training.tutorialDeployTemplatesStep4: Review the agent's assigned skills and add or remove any as needed for your workflow
training.tutorialDeployTemplatesStep5: Activate the agent and test it in the Agent Playground before going live
training.tutorialGettingStartedDesc: Get oriented with the Dr. Claw dashboard, understand the navigation, and configure your practice profile for the first time.
training.tutorialGettingStartedStep1: Log in and explore the Clinical Command Center dashboard — review the sidebar navigation to locate Agents, Skills, Integrations, and Settings
training.tutorialGettingStartedStep2: Navigate to Practice Profile and enter your organization name, specialty, address, and contact details
training.tutorialGettingStartedStep3: Visit Settings to configure your timezone, notification preferences, and default language
training.tutorialGettingStartedStep4: Review the Training Center (you're here!) to plan your learning path through beginner, intermediate, and advanced tutorials
training.tutorialHrDesc: Deploy and configure HR-focused agents to automate staff scheduling, credential tracking, onboarding, and workforce analytics.
training.tutorialHrStep1: Deploy an HR agent from the template gallery or create one with the HR & Staffing skill set
training.tutorialHrStep2: Configure the staff directory integration to sync employee records, roles, and schedules
training.tutorialHrStep3: Set up credential tracking to monitor license expirations, certifications, and required training
training.tutorialHrStep4: Create automated onboarding workflows for new hires — including document collection and system access setup
training.tutorialHrStep5: Configure workforce analytics dashboards to track staffing levels, overtime, and schedule coverage
training.tutorialHrStep6: Set up automated alerts for upcoming credential expirations and required compliance training deadlines
training.tutorialIntegrationsDesc: Link your Electronic Health Records system, billing platforms, and communication tools to power your AI agents with live data.
training.tutorialIntegrationsStep1: Navigate to EHR & Integrations from the sidebar and review the list of available connectors
training.tutorialIntegrationsStep2: Select your EHR system (e.g., Epic, Cerner, Athena) and follow the OAuth or API key setup flow
training.tutorialIntegrationsStep3: Configure data sync frequency — choose real-time, hourly, or daily depending on your needs
training.tutorialIntegrationsStep4: Map EHR fields to Dr. Claw data fields (patient name, DOB, insurance ID, etc.)
training.tutorialIntegrationsStep5: Test the integration by pulling a sample patient record and verify the data appears correctly
training.tutorialKnowledgeBaseDesc: Upload, organize, and link clinical documents to your agents so they can reference SOPs, protocols, and policies when responding to queries.
training.tutorialKnowledgeBaseStep1: Navigate to Medical Knowledge Base and review the existing document library organized by category
training.tutorialKnowledgeBaseStep2: Upload a new document — enter the name, select a category (Clinical Protocols, SOPs, Compliance, etc.), and add tags
training.tutorialKnowledgeBaseStep3: Link documents to specific agents using the Agent Knowledge Links section at the bottom of the page
training.tutorialKnowledgeBaseStep4: Search and filter documents by category, file type, or keyword to quickly locate resources
training.tutorialKnowledgeBaseStep5: Review agent access counts to see which documents are most frequently referenced by your AI agents
training.tutorialPatientPortalDesc: Configure the patient-facing portal to manage appointments, messages, care plans, and provide patients with secure access to their information.
training.tutorialPatientPortalStep1: Navigate to Patient Portal to see the patient directory with demographics, insurance, and care plan data
training.tutorialPatientPortalStep2: Review patient profiles — click on a patient to see their appointment history, messages, and assigned care team
training.tutorialPatientPortalStep3: Manage appointment scheduling — view the calendar, book new appointments, and handle rescheduling requests
training.tutorialPatientPortalStep4: Configure automated patient notifications for appointment reminders, follow-up care, and prescription refills
training.tutorialPatientPortalStep5: Review patient satisfaction metrics and feedback collected through automated surveys
training.tutorialPhiDesc: Configure Protected Health Information monitoring, zone isolation enforcement, and compliance controls to meet HIPAA requirements.
training.tutorialPhiStep1: Navigate to PHI Monitor to see the real-time dashboard showing PHI detection events across all zones
training.tutorialPhiStep2: Review zone isolation rules — verify that patient data cannot flow from Zone 1 (Clinical) to Zone 3 (External)
training.tutorialPhiStep3: Configure PHI detection sensitivity — set thresholds for automatic flagging of patient identifiers in messages
training.tutorialPhiStep4: Set up automatic redaction rules for outbound communications that may contain PHI
training.tutorialPhiStep5: Enable compliance alerts to notify administrators when potential HIPAA violations are detected
training.tutorialPhiStep6: Review the compliance scorecard to see your organization's current HIPAA compliance rating
training.tutorialPhiStep7: Export a compliance report for your records or to share with your compliance officer
training.tutorialPlaygroundDesc: Use the Agent Playground sandbox to test agent responses, debug configurations, and validate behavior before deploying to production.
training.tutorialPlaygroundStep1: Navigate to Agent Playground from the sidebar to access the sandboxed testing environment
training.tutorialPlaygroundStep2: Select an agent to test from the dropdown — the playground loads the agent's current configuration
training.tutorialPlaygroundStep3: Send test messages to simulate patient interactions and review the agent's responses in real time
training.tutorialPlaygroundStep4: Check the debug panel to see which skills the agent activated and how it processed each request
training.tutorialPlaygroundStep5: Adjust agent settings directly from the playground and re-test until the behavior meets expectations
training.tutorialReportsDesc: Create, schedule, and export reports covering agent performance, patient metrics, task completion, and compliance status.
training.tutorialReportsStep1: Navigate to Clinical Reports and browse available report templates (Agent Performance, Patient Volume, Task Metrics)
training.tutorialReportsStep2: Select a report template and configure the date range, agent filters, and data dimensions
training.tutorialReportsStep3: Preview the generated report with interactive charts showing trends and key performance indicators
training.tutorialReportsStep4: Schedule recurring reports to be generated and emailed to stakeholders automatically
training.tutorialReportsStep5: Export reports in PDF or CSV format for board presentations or compliance documentation
training.tutorialTaskTrackerDesc: Use the Kanban-style task tracker to create, assign, prioritize, and monitor clinical and operational tasks across your agent team.
training.tutorialTaskTrackerStep1: Navigate to Clinical Tasks to see the Kanban board with columns: To Do, In Progress, Review, and Done
training.tutorialTaskTrackerStep2: Create a new task — set the title, description, priority (Low/Medium/High/Urgent), and due date
training.tutorialTaskTrackerStep3: Assign the task to a specific agent or team member using the assignee dropdown
training.tutorialTaskTrackerStep4: Drag tasks between columns as they progress, or use the status dropdown for quick updates
training.tutorialTaskTrackerStep5: Use filters to view tasks by priority, assignee, or due date — and check overdue task alerts
training.tutorialTeamDesc: Add team members, assign roles and permissions, and configure access levels to ensure the right people manage the right agents.
training.tutorialTeamStep1: Navigate to Care Team from the sidebar and review the current team roster
training.tutorialTeamStep2: Click 'Invite Member' to add a new team member — enter their email and select a role (Admin, Manager, Viewer)
training.tutorialTeamStep3: Configure role-based permissions — define which agents, settings, and data each role can access
training.tutorialTeamStep4: Set up department groups to organize team members by function (Clinical, Billing, Front Office, IT)
training.tutorialTeamStep5: Review the team activity log to monitor who made changes to agents, settings, or patient data
training.tutorialVoiceDesc: Set up voice-based interactions for agents — including speech-to-text transcription, voice commands, and audio verification workflows.
training.tutorialVoiceStep1: Navigate to an agent's settings and enable Voice Input under the Communication Channels section
training.tutorialVoiceStep2: Configure the speech-to-text engine — select language, dialect, and medical terminology recognition
training.tutorialVoiceStep3: Set up voice command shortcuts for common actions (e.g., 'Schedule appointment' or 'Check insurance')
training.tutorialVoiceStep4: Test the voice input by recording a sample phrase and verifying the transcription accuracy
training.tutorialVoiceStep5: Enable voice verification for sensitive actions — requiring spoken confirmation before processing PHI requests
training.tutorialWorkflowsDesc: Design multi-step automated workflows that chain agent actions together — from patient intake to follow-up care coordination.
training.tutorialWorkflowsStep1: Navigate to Care Workflows and click 'Create Workflow' to open the visual workflow builder
training.tutorialWorkflowsStep2: Add a trigger step — choose from options like 'New Patient Registration', 'Appointment Booked', or 'Lab Results Received'
training.tutorialWorkflowsStep3: Chain action steps by dragging agents onto the canvas — each agent performs its task and passes data to the next
training.tutorialWorkflowsStep4: Add conditional branches to handle different scenarios (e.g., if insurance is verified vs. denied)
training.tutorialWorkflowsStep5: Test the workflow with sample data to verify each step executes correctly before activating

workflow.createDialogDescription: Define the steps, agents, and triggers for your new workflow.
workflow.subtitle: Design, automate, and monitor multi-step agent workflows
workflow.title: Workflow Builder
workflow.toastAddAtLeastOneStep: Please add at least one step with a name and agent.
workflow.toastCannotRemoveDesc: A workflow must have at least one step.
workflow.toastWorkflowCreatedDesc: The workflow "{{name}}" has been created successfully.
workflow.zoneViolationClinicalToExternal: Step {{stepA}} ({{agentA}}) is Clinical but Step {{stepB}} ({{agentB}}) is External — data cannot flow from Clinical to External.
workflow.zoneViolationExternalToClinical: Step {{stepA}} ({{agentA}}) is External but Step {{stepB}} ({{agentB}}) is Clinical — data cannot flow from External to Clinical.

commandStation.subtitle: Communicate with agents, issue instructions, and monitor activity in real time
commandStation.title: Command Station

footer.description: The AI agent platform built exclusively for healthcare. Automate patient scheduling, clinical documentation, insurance verification, care coordination, and every operation in your medical practice.

cta.footer: 14-day free trial · No credit card required · HIPAA secured
cta.subtitle: Join thousands of healthcare providers using Dr. Claw AI agents to automate clinical operations, reduce no-shows, and deliver better patient care.
cta.title: Ready to Transform Your Practice?

faq.a1: Yes. Dr. Claw is fully HIPAA compliant with end-to-end encryption, PHI protection, and we offer Business Associate Agreements (BAAs) on Professional plans and above. All data is processed in SOC 2 certified infrastructure.
faq.a2: You create an agent by giving it a name, selecting one or more skills (like CEO, CMO, Grant Writer, or Clinical Documentation), and choosing which AI model powers it. Each agent operates independently and can be customized with specific instructions and workflows.
faq.a3: Absolutely. On Professional plans and above, you can assign different LLM providers to each agent — OpenAI, Claude, Gemini, MiniMax, Kimi, or Mistral. Switch models anytime to find the best fit for each task.
faq.a4: We offer 100+ skills including C-suite roles (CEO, CFO, CMO, CAIO, CIO, COO, CHRO), Professional Copywriter, Grant Writer, Researcher, full Marketing & Sales Suites, and 20+ healthcare-specific skills covering clinical documentation, patient management, billing, referrals, and more. All starter-tier skills are included from day one.
faq.a5: Yes. We integrate with major EHR/EMR platforms and also support connections to AWS Healthcare, Notion, Airtable, Slack, and other productivity tools. On Advanced and Enterprise plans, we can build custom integrations for your specific systems.
faq.a6: Most users deploy their first agent in under 5 minutes. We provide 16 pre-built templates that work out of the box — just select a template, connect your API key, and your agent is live. Custom agents take only a few minutes more.
faq.a7: All data is encrypted at rest and in transit using AES-256 and TLS 1.3. We maintain SOC 2 Type II certification, conduct regular penetration testing, and follow the principle of least privilege. Your API keys are stored in encrypted vaults and never shared across accounts.
faq.a8: The 14-day free trial gives you full access to the Professional plan — 25 agents, all 60+ skills, multi-LLM support, full voice AI, and all integrations including EHR/ERP. No credit card required to start. At the end of the trial, choose the plan that fits your needs.
faq.q3: Can I switch between AI models?
faq.q5: Does Dr. Claw integrate with my EHR/EMR system?
faq.q6: How quickly can I deploy an agent?
faq.q8: What does the free trial include?
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Dr. Claw.

features.clinicalBuilderDesc: Create AI agents for front desk, clinical coordination, patient outreach, and every role in your practice.
features.docsDesc: AI-generated clinical notes, referral letters, prior authorizations, and patient communications.
features.ehrDesc: Connect to Epic, Cerner, athenahealth, and 40+ healthcare integrations. Bring your own API keys.
features.hipaaFirstDesc: HIPAA compliant, BAA available, SOC 2 certified, PHI auto-redaction, and end-to-end encryption.
features.medicalSkillsDesc: Patient scheduling, insurance verification, clinical documentation, referral management, and more.
features.multiLlmDesc: OpenAI, Claude, Gemini, MiniMax, Kimi — choose the best model for each clinical workflow.
features.subtitle: A complete AI platform purpose-built for medical practices, clinics, and health systems — from patient intake to clinical operations.
features.teamDesc: Multi-provider access, role-based permissions, and shared agent workflows across your practice.
features.templatesDesc: Pre-built agents for front desk, clinical coordinator, insurance verifier, post-op care, and more.
features.voiceAiDesc: Voice agents for patient calls, appointment reminders, post-op follow-ups, and clinical dictation.

howItWorks.step1Desc: One-step registration gets your clinic an instant HIPAA-compliant workspace. No setup calls.
howItWorks.step2Desc: Choose from healthcare templates or build custom agents. Assign medical skills and connect your EHR.
howItWorks.step3Desc: Your agents start automating scheduling, documentation, and outreach immediately across your practice.
howItWorks.subtitle: Three simple steps to automate your healthcare practice.

integrations.note: 55+ integrations available · Custom integrations on Enterprise plans
integrations.subtitle: Bring your own API keys and connect your preferred providers. Full control, zero lock-in.

personas.executiveDesc: Strategic planning, financial analysis, and AI strategy from your C-suite AI team.
personas.grantDesc: Grant writing, market research, and competitive intelligence — automated.
personas.healthcareDesc: AI front desk, clinical documentation, and patient follow-ups — purpose-built for healthcare.
personas.hrDesc: Talent management, process optimization, and compliance handled by AI agents.
personas.marketingBullet1: Blog posts, social media & ad copy
personas.marketingDesc: Content creation, campaign strategy, and brand management powered by AI agents.
personas.multiDesc: Deploy AI agents across your entire organization with shared libraries and role-based access.
personas.subtitle: From solo practitioners to multi-location health systems — Dr. Claw agents automate every department in your organization.

pricing.advDesc: For large organizations with unlimited agents, custom integrations, and white-label.
pricing.entDesc: For health systems and hospital networks with custom deployment and SLA needs.
pricing.proDesc: For growing practices that need multi-model AI, full voice, and EHR integration.
pricing.starterDesc: For solo practitioners and small clinics getting started with AI-powered operations.
pricing.subtitle: Works alongside your EHR/ERP — never replaces it. Unlimited skills, training & project management included. No per-provider fees.
pricing.trialNote: 14-day free trial on all plans. No credit card required.

testimonials.q1: Dr. Claw's AI front desk agent reduced our no-show rate by 34% in the first month. The voice follow-ups feel completely natural to our patients.
testimonials.q2: The CAIO skill gave us a complete AI adoption roadmap in hours. It would have taken our team weeks to produce the same strategic analysis.
testimonials.q3: Referral tracking used to be a nightmare. Now our AI agent handles the entire loop — from sending referrals to confirming appointments to follow-up.
testimonials.q4: We replaced three separate content tools with one Dr. Claw marketing agent. Blog posts, email campaigns, social media — all from a single dashboard.
testimonials.q5: Running five agents across our practice — front desk, billing, clinical docs, patient outreach, and research. The multi-agent system is a game changer.
testimonials.q6: Our grant writer agent drafted a $2.4M NIH proposal that reviewers praised for its clarity. The research skill pulled supporting data we would have missed.
testimonials.subtitle: See how organizations across healthcare, tech, and nonprofits are deploying AI agents with Dr. Claw.
testimonials.t3: Dir. of Operations, Sunrise Surgical Center

unified.bannerDesc: From patient intake to post-op follow-ups, every clinical workflow lives inside one secure, HIPAA-compliant environment. No sprawl. No risk. Just a healthier practice.
unified.bannerTitle: OpenClaw — The Unified Healthcare AI Platform
unified.demoTitle: See How Agents, Data & Workflows Connect
unified.pillarDeployDesc: Launch pre-built clinical agents in minutes. No lengthy onboarding, no IT overhead, no vendor lock-in.
unified.pillarHealthcareDesc: Purpose-built for medical practices, clinics, and health systems — not a generic AI tool retrofitted for healthcare.
unified.pillarHipaaDesc: Every workflow, agent, and data flow is built to meet HIPAA standards out of the box — no configuration required.
unified.pillarTeamDesc: Give every provider, coordinator, and front-desk staff their own AI agent — all under one secure, unified environment.
unified.pillarWorkspaceDesc: One platform for scheduling, documentation, care coordination, billing outreach, and more — no stitching tools together.
unified.pillarZoneDesc: PHI is locked inside dedicated security zones. Your patient data never mingles with other tenants or leaves your environment.
unified.subtitle: OpenClaw brings your clinical AI agents, patient workflows, and healthcare integrations into a single, unified environment — secured end-to-end so you can focus on care, not compliance.
